claim: Set up end-to-end tracing for all agent steps (LLM calls, tool executions, latency, errors) to enable production debugging when agents fail in non-obvious ways
actionable: Instrument your agent code with tracing via LangSmith/similar; capture every LLM call, tool invocation, and latency metric; use tracing UI to drill into failure traces
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Build guardrails (gate queries before main agent logic) as a first step in agent architecture to block malicious or out-of-scope requests before they consume tokens
actionable: Add an LLM guard that classifies incoming queries as in-scope or out-of-scope before running your main agent; log guard rejections for later analysis
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Use waterfall/latency views in traces to identify if tools are running sequentially (slow) vs in parallel (fast) and optimize tool calling patterns accordingly
actionable: Visualize latency breakdowns per tool call; check if tools could be batched; refactor agent prompts to encourage parallel tool execution
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Monitor agent performance over multi-turn conversations by viewing thread-level traces; agents often struggle as context window grows, revealing degradation patterns
actionable: In your tracing tool, switch to thread view for full conversations; compare early-turn vs late-turn agent quality; identify context-window degradation
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Use LLM-as-judge online evaluators with sampling rates to measure agent quality in production without running full evals (10% sample = cost control)
actionable: Create LLM evaluators that score production traces on dimensions like helpfulness (1-10); set sampling rate to 10-50% to control cost; filter high-value traces
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Evaluate sub-agents and tool calls independently (not just end-to-end) by filtering on specific run names to catch intermediate failures masked by final output
actionable: Create separate evaluators for guardrail checks, tool selection, and final response; compare performance per component
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Use thread-level evaluators to check if agents accomplished the original user goal across a full conversation, not just semantic correctness of individual responses
actionable: Set up an evaluator that looks at the entire conversation thread and judges if the user's problem was actually solved (boolean: resolved/not-resolved)
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Curate golden datasets by selecting real production traces, having SMEs annotate them, and manually fixing/editing agent output to define ground truth
actionable: Export 10-50 traces from production; have domain experts review and edit agent responses to the form you want; save edited versions as reference outputs in a dataset
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Run offline experiments comparing multiple models/prompts on the same golden dataset; track latency, cost, and quality metrics to identify Pareto-optimal tradeoffs
actionable: In LangSmith, run experiments with Haiku, GPT-4-mini, Sonnet on your golden data; compare cost vs quality; use baselines to detect regressions
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Use insights clustering to automatically categorize failure modes across thousands of traces, revealing unexpected usage patterns and edge cases
actionable: Run an insights report on production traces grouped by failure type; let AI cluster them; discover which question categories cause most errors
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Set up automations to send high-performing traces (e.g., helpfulness >= 8) to annotation queues for SME review and inclusion in future training datasets
actionable: Create a filter rule: if feedback.helpfulness >= 8, auto-add to annotation queue; SMEs review and move to golden dataset; this feeds your next experiment cycle
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Improve prompts using insights from production traces by pulling failing traces into a prompt playground and iterating with LLM-suggested improvements (XML tags, examples)
actionable: Find a low-quality trace; pull it into the prompt playground; ask an LLM to improve the system prompt with best practices (XML, few-shots); test against the trace
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Use trajectory evaluation to ensure agents call the right sub-agents in the right order before judging final output; catches routing failures invisible in end-to-end traces
actionable: Create evaluators that check: (1) did agent call the right tool? (2) in the right order? (3) with correct arguments? Run these before checking final output quality
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Set deployment monitoring dashboards to track trace metrics, LLM call counts, latency by tool, error rates, and feedback scores over time to catch performance regressions
actionable: Enable monitoring for your deployed agent; watch dashboards for dips in helpfulness, spikes in error rate, or increases in latency; alert on anomalies
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Use Poly (LLM-powered chat UI in LangSmith) to filter traces and generate insights conversationally rather than manually navigating UI
actionable: Instead of manually building filter queries, ask Poly: 'Show me traces from the last 2 days' or 'Which evaluator found the most errors?'
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Collect user feedback (thumbs up/down) on deployed agent responses to automatically flag low-satisfaction traces for SME review and improvement iteration
actionable: Add thumbs-up/down buttons to agent responses; route thumbs-down responses to a dedicated annotation queue for root cause analysis and prompt refinement
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith

claim: Start dataset creation from production traces (not synthetic data) because agents produce realistic outputs you can edit; easier than generating ground truth from scratch
actionable: Avoid synthetic data generation; instead run your agent on 50 real queries, annotate the outputs, and use agent-produced text as starting point for golden dataset
source_title: How to Debug, Evaluate, and Ship Reliable AI Agents with LangSmith
